content.problemStatement:
You are given two binary strings AAA and BBB of length NNN. You are allowed to perform the following operations any number of times (possibly zero):


Choose any index iii and swap the characters AiA_iAi‚Äč and BiB_iBi‚Äč.
Choose any two indices iii and jjj and simultaneously swap AiA_iAi‚Äč with AjA_jAj‚Äč and BiB_iBi‚Äč with BjB_jBj‚Äč.
Determine if it is possible to perform a sequence of these operations such that both AAA and BBB become palindromes.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines:

The first line of each test case contains NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the strings.
The second line contains the binary string AAA.
The third line contains the binary string BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing`YES` if it is possible to make both strings palindromic using the given operations, or `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YES`, `yEs`, `yes`, and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 2: There can be many other ways; here is one:

Choose i=1i=1i=1 and swap A1A_1A1‚Äč and B1B_1B1‚Äč, which gives A=00A=00A=00 and B=11B=11B=11.

Test Case 3: It can be proven that, no matter what operations we apply, we cannot make both AAA and BBB palindromes.
Test Case 4: There can be many other ways; here is one:

Choose i=2i=2i=2 and j=3j=3j=3 and swap A2A_2A2‚Äč with A3A_3A3‚Äč and B2B_2B2‚Äč with B3B_3B3‚Äč, which gives A=101A=101A=101 and B=101B=101B=101.

Test Case 5: There can be many other ways; here is one:

Choose i=3i=3i=3 and j=4j=4j=4 and swap A2A_2A2‚Äč with A3A_3A3‚Äč and B2B_2B2‚Äč with B3B_3B3‚Äč, which gives A=1000A=1000A=1000 and B=0001B=0001B=0001.
Choose i=4i=4i=4 and swap A4A_4A4‚Äč and B4B_4B4‚Äč, which gives A=1001A=1001A=1001 and B=0000B=0000B=0000.
